senses.definition: partially or somewhat expository; containing some elements of exposition but not fully explanatory
examples: The teacher gave a semiexpository lecture, mixing facts with discussion.
A semiexpository essay provides background before presenting the main argument.

senses.definition: partial or limited exposure to something, not full or complete
examples: The study showed semiexposure to the chemical; more tests are needed.
Semiexposure to sunlight reduced the risk of certain skin issues in the experiment.

senses.definition: partially in the style of expressionism; having some expressive, impressionistic qualities
examples: The painting is semiexpressionistic, blending clear forms with loose, emotive brushwork.
Her semiexpressionistic prose hints at emotions without full description.

senses.definition: partially external; not entirely internal or domestic
examples: The device has semiexternal connections to external sensors.
Semiexternal components were added to the prototype for testing.

senses.definition: made partially external or expressed outwardly; treated as externally visible in part
examples: The policy was semiexternalized to gain public support.
The data was semiexternalized to wheels of the project for transparency.

senses.definition: to a partially external extent; in a manner that is partly outward-facing
examples: The system communicates semiexternally with partner networks.
Semiexternally, the project keeps some data on local servers.

senses.definition: almost extinct; very near to extinction but not completely
examples: The species is semiextinct in the wild, with only a few individuals remaining.
Conservation efforts are keeping the population semiextinct rather than fully extinct.

senses.definition: almost extinction; a state close to extinction
examples: The semiextinction of the species prompted urgent intervention.
Researchers warn that semiextinction could become full extinction without action.

senses.definition: partly admirable or worthy of praise; somewhat admirable
examples: The performance was semifable, with moments of brilliance.
Her semifable actions inspired the team, even if not perfect.

senses.definition: partly fabulous or extraordinarily good in some aspects
examples: The show was semifabulous, dazzling in costumes but uneven in pacing.
Their semifabulous performance won cheers from the crowd.

senses.definition: a partial failure; an attempt that did not fully succeed
examples: The project was a semifailure due to budget constraints.
Despite a semifailure, the team learned valuable lessons.

senses.definition: a light form of fantasy or dream-like imagery; not fully famine-related but a coined term suggesting partial famine context
examples: The term semifamine appeared in the essay as a stylistic metaphor.
In the novel, semifamine imagery underscores scarcity without actual famine.

senses.definition: a partial band or fascia; a structure that is halfway between fasciae in anatomy or botany
examples: The specimen shows a semifascia separating tissue layers.
Biologists describe a semifascia as a partial connective band.

senses.definition: partially connected by a fascia or band; somewhat linked
examples: The semifasciated fibers show partial fusion.
Semifasciated elements were observed in the specimen.

senses.definition: a style or trend that is partially fashionable; not fully on-trend
examples: The collection is semifashion, balancing classic cuts with contemporary details.
Her semifashion sense leans toward vintage with a modern twist.

senses.definition: partly fast or abstaining from some foods but not completely fasting
examples: The diet is semifast, allowing certain meals while restricting others.
During the challenge, participants follow a semifast regimen.

senses.definition: partially fatalistic; showing some fatalistic attitudes but not fully
examples: His semifatalistic outlook hindered proactive planning.
The coach urged a semifatalistic mindset to avoid resignation.

senses.definition: partially wild or feral; not fully domesticated
examples: The animal exhibited semiferal behavior after years in captivity.
The semiferal dog roamed the outskirts of the village.

senses.definition: bearing or producing bearing; used in biology to describe structures that bear seeds or spores
examples: The plant has semififerous glands that release seeds slowly.
Semiferous tissues were studied under the microscope.

senses.definition: partially feudal; showing feudal characteristics in part rather than fully
examples: The organization exists in semifeudal structures with hereditary roles.
A semifeudal system can limit workers' mobility.

senses.definition: a system or condition that is partly feudal in structure or practice
examples: Scholars debated the presence of semifeudalism in the region's past.
The transition away from semifeudalism altered social hierarchies.

senses.definition: a partial fib; a minor lie that is not fully fabricated
examples: He told a semifib about his whereabout to avoid trouble.
The story contained a semifib to soften the truth.

senses.definition: partly fiction; a work that blends fact and fiction
examples: The memoir reads as semifiction, weaving real events with imaginative details.
Publishers categorize it as semifiction to emphasize storytelling.

senses.definition: partially fictional; containing both factual and fictional elements
examples: The author uses semifictional scenes to illustrate real experiences.
A semifictional approach can engage readers with authentic emotion.

senses.definition: made partially fictional; converted into semifiction
examples: The diary was semifictionalized to protect privacy.
The episode semifictionalized real events for drama.
